description.example: This schema describes a b2c customer profile.

items.description: Key of a field that is required in the schema.

properties.description: Object describing each field in your desired schema. In this object, each property is a new object, describing the field according to: `type` (string); `sensitive` (boolean); `pii` (boolean) and `items.type` (if field is array).

additionalProperties.description: Schema of any given field described in the `properties` field.

sensitive.description: Indicates whether the property is sensitive data. Set to `true` if `pii` is `true` and set to `false` if `pii` is `false`.

pii.description: Indicates whether the property is Personal Identifiable Information (PII).

items.description: Object containing the type of the items if the field is an array. Typically, arrays will contain strings and will be used for fields such as `email`.

documentTTL.description: Document time to live, in days. After this many days from its creation or update, any document cerated from this schema will be deleted.

v-indexed.description: List of fields to be indexed.

v-unique.description: List of fields that must be unique.
